text: Markets chose violence again to close out the week.

Dear S&P 500 Committee,

You added $SMCI at the top and then watched it lose ~80% (allegedly with a little help from a hair dryer).

You have the chance to swap that out for $SOFI which has a banking license, 13.7M members and is guiding for at least 30% CAGR through 2028 (all without ever needing heat tools to pass an audit). 

We think you know what to do.

$ONDS was named a strategic investor in $UMAC $150M public offering priced at $17 per share.

Unusual Machines makes key drone components for the U.S. supply chain reinforcing Ondas push to build exposure across the broader autonomous systems stack.

$NVDA confirmed a deal to supply $AMZN AWS with 1 million GPUs through 2027. 

The deal also includes Spectrum networking and ConnectX infrastructure showing how spending is expanding beyond GPUs into the full AI stack.

$AMD says NEC is building Japan’s first optical communication satellite constellation using AMD technology.

The project will use AMD Versal adaptive SoCs for high-speed routing and signal processing in space to help improve connectivity on Earth.

Imagine being Michael Dell right now.

One of your biggest competitors in $SMCI may have just handed you billions of dollars after getting caught committing fraud with a hair dryer.

$ASTS just hired a Chief of AI & Autonomy (former $BA AI lab director) confirms commercial ramp is close enough that autonomous network management now matters.

Beam coverage, power allocation & interference get much harder to manage manually across a large satellite network.

$ONDS Q4 PRELIMINARY RESULTS

• Revenue: $30M vs. Est. $28M
• EBITDA: ($10M) vs. Est. ($9M)
• Net Income: $83M vs. Est. ($10M) boosted by a $102M warrant gain
• Cash position: ~$1.6B 

FY26 Guidance
• Revenue: $175M vs. Est. $174M (excludes all 2026 acquisitions)

$SMCI is now down 27% after prosecutors charged co-founder in alleged scheme to smuggle $2.5B of $NVDA servers to China.

Prosecutors say he used a Southeast Asia shell company, fake servers & swapped serial labels with a hair dryer.

Another brutal chapter for a company that just cannot stay out of its own way.

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang says orbital data centers are feasible but face cooling constraints as space requires radiation-based heat dissipation instead of traditional methods.

He adds that Nvidia is already running CUDA in satellites and is exploring space-based compute.

$TSLA is reportedly in talks to purchase ~$2.9B of solar manufacturing equipment from Chinese suppliers. 

The move supports Tesla’s push toward ~100GW of U.S. solar capacity by the end of 2028.

$AMZN is reportedly building a new mobile phone which would be its first since the 2014 Fire Phone.

The device internally dubbed “Transformer” is designed as a personalized AI hub centered around Alexa.

$NVDA responds to the $SMCI report emphasizing strict compliance and close coordination with customers and regulators as export controls expand.

It adds that unlawful diversion of U.S. chips to China is a losing proposition and receives no support or service from Nvidia.
U.S. prosecutors charge three individuals tied to $SMCI (including a co-founder) in an alleged scheme to smuggle $NVDA chips to China.

$AMZN has reportedly acquired autonomous robotics startup Rivr.

Another sign Amazon is pushing deeper into automating the physical layer of commerce and logistics.

$NET CEO Matthew Prince says AI bot traffic is on pace to surpass human internet traffic by 2027.

He notes that AI agents can hit thousands of sites for a single task creating far more load than a human user and putting new pressure on internet infrastructure.

This capital exodus exemplifies the Laffer Curve in action, where higher tax rates beyond an optimal point reduce total revenue, a concept validated by economic studies like those from the Tax Foundation showing similar effects in high-tax jurisdictions.

Love our Treasury Sec!

Iranian oil has been sanctioned for many years. The 140 million barrels he is referring to are part of the "dark fleet" that has been shipping Iranian oil to Asia (mainly China) for years. Iran gets money for oil, and China buys it at a discount.

So, this oil already exists in the world market. It doesn't matter who buys it, or under what scheme (sanctioned through a dark fleet or legitimate open market sale), as a barrel sold anywhere, for whatever reason, adds to world supply.

To emphasize, these barrels are already part of the world supply. These barrels are not randomly floating in a tanker on some ocean (for years?), waiting for sanctions to be lifted before they can be sold and offloaded.

By allowing oil to be unsanctioned, all Bessent is doing is allowing Iranian oil to be sold to places like Japan or South Korea. In other words, all he did was raise the price of Iranian oil because they now have more bidders.

He just made Iran wealthier and did nothing for the price of crude oil.

Uber $UBER and Nvidia $NVDA announced that they will deploy a fleet of robotaxis using Nvidia’s DRIVE Hyperion autonomous driving platform and Alpamayo LLM, starting in Los Angeles and San Francisco in 2027, and expanding to 28 cities worldwide by 2028, per Reuters.

While Nvidia’s $NVDA $1 trillion in AI chip visibility through 2027 may seem like the key takeaway from GTC, I’d argue there was another jaw-dropping stat intended to set the stage in the coming years. 

Although this stat has not seen the recognition it deserves, it foreshadows higher revenue as we exit the decade.
